rawtext.text:
Awoken. Good. Saves me walking to find you.

Roderic Hale, captain of the Elderfall watch — all nine of them, four of whom held a spear for the first time last Tuesday. I count days, I drill farmers, and I keep one eye on the hills. §cEvery twentieth day, the seal exhales and the Corrupted march on this square.§r I intend for that to keep being their mistake.
§eWhat happened on your last twentieth day?§r

I served twelve years in the lowland legions. I have stood shield-lines against cavalry, fire, and worse. Nothing — §onothing§r — is like the twentieth day.

They don't shout. They don't break. They come up the hill in step, humming that one low note, and the ones you cut down in the last outbreak are in the front rank of the next one, wearing the same wounds.

Last cycle we lost the west gate for six minutes. Brenna's mail is the only reason I'm here complaining about it. So when I ask you for iron and dead raiders, understand: I am not decorating. §cI am counting days.§r

In order of how much sleep they cost me:

§6The Silent Village§r — Havenbrook, our sister village southwest, has gone quiet. Walk its gate and its well. Go armed.

§6Steel for the Watch§r — §f20 iron ingots§r and §f12 leather§r so Brenna can armor my militia. First suit off the racks is yours.

§6The Captain's Gambit§r — thin their muster: §f15 Corrupted Raiders§r, before the next twentieth day.

§6Embermarch§r — be in the square when the next outbreak breaks. Be standing when it's done. Dawnbreaker is waiting for whoever manages it.
